core_mechanism: a threshold contagion/contact spreading process on a network with a heavy-tailed (power-law) degree distribution, whose diverging degree fluctuations drive the critical spreading threshold to zero so that any nonzero transmission rate produces a persistent outbreak
brief_reason: The abstract defines a dynamical model for the spreading of infections on scale-free networks and finds the absence of an epidemic threshold and its associated critical behavior, which is exactly a threshold spreading process suppressed by the network's heavy-tailed connectivity.
